title: Road Lines
description: What the painted lines mean: passing, direction, lane changes, and where to stop.

scenarios.prompt: Tap the road where you are allowed to pass.

feedback.correct: Yes! A dashed yellow line means you may pass when it's safe.
feedback.solid: A solid yellow line means no passing. The dashed line is where you can pass. Try again.
feedback.default: You may pass where the yellow line is dashed. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: Tap the road where cars go in opposite directions.

feedback.correct: Right! A yellow line separates cars going opposite ways.
feedback.white: Those cars go the same way — that's a white line. Yellow lines separate opposite directions. Try again.
feedback.default: Yellow lines separate traffic going opposite ways. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: Tap the road where passing is NOT allowed.

feedback.correct: Yes — two solid yellow lines mean no passing in either direction.
feedback.dash: The dashed line is where you CAN pass. The double solid lines mean no passing. Try again.
feedback.default: Double solid yellow lines mean no passing. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: Both roads go one way. Tap the one where you may change lanes.

feedback.correct: Yes — a dashed white line means you may change lanes when it's safe.
feedback.solid: A solid white line means stay in your lane. The dashed line is where you may change. Try again.
feedback.default: You may change lanes where the white line is dashed. Try again.

scenarios.concept: Where to stop at a crosswalk
scenarios.prompt: Tap where your car should stop.

feedback.correct: Yes! Stop at the white line, before the crosswalk. That keeps the walkway clear for people.
feedback.onwalk: That's the crosswalk, where people walk. Stop just before it, at the white line. Try again.
feedback.past: That's too far — you'd be in the intersection. Stop at the white line before the crosswalk. Try again.
feedback.default: Stop at the white line before the crosswalk. Try again.
